texto: He cut away the insignificant and incidental. Those bits were tossedaway from the juicy core pieces. He slid the excess tissue and tendons away and reorganized the good bits on his plate. Those pieces were translated into words and catchy phrases and assembled like a new life.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Jeffry R. Halverson: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de sentido da existência e experiência vivida. O trecho “He cut away the insignificant and incidental. Those bits were tossedaway from the juicy core piec…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, writers-on-writing, writing pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de termino e writing.

frase_original: He cut away the insignificant and incidental. Those bits were tossedaway from the juicy core pieces. He slid the excess tissue and tendons away and reorganized the good bits on his plate. Those pieces were translated into words and catchy phrases and assembled like a new life.

texto: It portrayed motherhood as the highest position that a woman could achieve. For God had made Mary neither a prophet nor the messiah nor the daughter of God. Nor did God take the form of a woman. She was only the womb. She was a perpetual virgin too, and she endured the vilest harassment because of it, or so the story went. Rebecca couldn’t relate to the Virgin Mary at all.
explicacao: Citação de Jeffry R. Halverson sobre temas ligados a fe. “It portrayed motherhood as the highest position that a woman could achieve. For God had made Mary…” articula fe, feminism, god em registro de fe, com relevância para contextos como termino e god.

frase_original: It portrayed motherhood as the highest position that a woman could achieve. For God had made Mary neither a prophet nor the messiah nor the daughter of God. Nor did God take the form of a woman. She was only the womb. She was a perpetual virgin too, and she endured the vilest harassment because of it, or so the story went. Rebecca couldn’t relate to the Virgin Mary at all.
